concepts.finding:
Our method substantially improves key business metrics,
measured in terms of clicks and conversions.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
We propose
to use a novel neural language-based algorithm speciﬁcally
tailored for delivering eﬀective product recommendations,
which was evaluated against baselines that included show-
ing popular products and products predicted based on co-
occurrence.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Ads in the form of product recommendations were success-
fully tested on online traﬃc, where we observed a steady
9% lift in click-through rates over other ad formats in mail,
as well as comparable lift in conversion rates.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
In this section
we propose a novel approach to simultaneously learn vec-
tor representations of products and users such that, given
a user, recommendations can be performed by ﬁnding K
nearest products in the joint embedding space.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Given predictions for a certain user, once the user logs into
the e-mail client we show a new recommendation after every
user action, including clicking on folders, composing e-mail,
and searching the inbox.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
In our ongoing
work, we plan to utilize implicit feedback available through
ad views, ad clicks, and conversions to further improve the
performance of our product recommendation system.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
Eﬀective
personalization and targeting [11], where the goal is to ﬁnd
the best matching ads to be displayed for each individual
user, is essential to tackling this problem, as ads need to be
highly relevant to overcome user’s inclination to focus nar-
rowly on the e-mail task.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:

often exhibits signiﬁcant limitations in practical tasks, suf-
fering from high dimensionality of the problem and severe
data sparsity, resulting in suboptimal performance.

and user-to-product recommendation models that make use
of the learned representations.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Finally, products from the top clusters are
sorted by their cosine similarity to p, and we use the top K
products as recommendations.
concepts.implication: Applicable to user/item clustering for personalization

One of the main advantages of the user2vec model is that
the product recommendations are speciﬁcally tailored for
that user based on his purchase history.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
Finally, we show
comparative results of various baseline recommendation al-
gorithms.

concepts.finding:
To achieve this we propose time-decayed scor-
ing of recommendations, followed by choice of top K prod-
ucts with the highest score.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The results show that discounting of old pre-
dictions leads to improved recommendation accuracy, with
decay factor of α = 0.9 being an optimal choice.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

sured the number of clicks on product ads that occurred after

of shown (or impressed) product ads.

results strongly suggest beneﬁts of the proposed approach

concepts.finding:
Given that a signiﬁcant percent-
age of overall traﬃc has commercial intent, a popular form
of targeted advertising is mail retargeting (MRT), where ad-
vertisers target users who previously received e-mails from
certain commercial web domains.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
A recent paper [14] proposed
to make use of this vast potential and presented a clustering
method to generate MRT rules, showing that such rules are
more accurate than the ones generated by human experts.
concepts.implication: Applicable to user/item clustering for personalization
